channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Bosnian Serb leader Dodik is convicted and sentenced for separatist activities as tensions surge
item.description: SARAJEVO, Bosnia-Herzegovina (AP) — A court in Bosnia on Wednesday sentenced the pro-Russia Bosnian Serb president, Milorad Dodik, to one year in prison and banned him from politics for six years over his separatist actions as tensions mount in the fragile Balkan state.
item.dc:creator: AMER COHADZIC, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Christianity in US stops decline after years of losses: Survey
item.description: Although down substantially from 2007, 44 percent of Americans said they pray at least once a day, a figure that has remained consistent in the last four years.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court seems likely to rule for Ohio woman claiming job bias because she's straight
item.description: The Supreme Court seems likely to side with an Ohio woman who claims she suffered sex discrimination in her employment because she is straight.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sherman, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Utah is one step away from becoming 1st state to ban fluoride in water
item.description: The Utah governor's signature is all that stands between a state ban on water fluoridation after House Bill 81 cleared its final hurdle in the legislature on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Drachman, Fox 5

item.title: WATCH: Officers cut open man's 'narco wig' at Colombian airport to reveal cocaine
item.description: He may have been using his head, but a 40-year-old passenger wasn't able to get past Colombian airport security with a cocaine-stuffed "narco wig," police said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5

item.title: New search to begin for Malaysia Airlines Flight 370: Reports
item.description: The latest search comes after the Malaysian government in December agreed to accept a second "no find, no fee" proposal from the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: A federal judge in Seattle blocks Trump’s effort to halt the refugee admissions system
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) — A federal judge in Seattle on Tuesday blocked President Donald Trump’s suspension of the nation’s refugee admissions system, saying that while the president has broad authority over who comes into the country, he cannot nullify the law passed by Congress establishing the program.
item.dc:creator: GENE JOHNSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Idaho prosecutors seek to limit Bryan Kohberger's alibi evidence at murder trial
item.description: BOISE, Idaho (AP) — Idaho prosecutors say a man charged in the stabbing deaths of four University of Idaho students shouldn't be allowed to offer an alibi defense unless he takes the stand himself during the murder trial later this year.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Scenes at US Capitol on war's anniversary reflect partisan divide on Ukraine
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — More than 100 House members at one point packed the steps of the U.S. Capitol to show their support for Ukraine following Russia's invasion three years ago, Republicans joining with Democrats in a forceful display of bipartisanship.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING and STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Princeton man shared eerie poems on social media before allegedly killing former star athlete brother, cat
item.description: The Princeton, New Jersey, man accused of killing his former college athlete brother and a cat posted eerie messages to social media months ago.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: American flag hung upside down in Yosemite National Park in protest over layoffs
item.description: Yosemite National Park staffers hung an American flag upside down from the park's El Capitan summit over the weekend to protest recent layoffs by the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: CARFAX expert tells you what to know before purchasing a car
item.description: Carfax's Editor-in-Chief Patrick Olsen reports that the agency monitors vehicle prices across seven categories.
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Joyce, NewsNation

item.title: Missouri woman accused in plot to sell Graceland says she'll plead guilty
item.description: A Missouri woman said Tuesday she is pleading guilty to a federal charge accusing her of concocting a brazen plot to defraud Elvis Presley’s family by trying to auction off his Graceland mansion and property before a judge halted the mysterious foreclosure sale.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: WATCH: Man steals 2 bulldog puppies as another 'fakes seizure' to distract staff
item.description: Two bulldog puppies were taken from a pet store in Colorado after a man "allegedly faked a seizure to distract employees," the Arapahoe Sheriff's Office said.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: FCC chairman climbs Nexstar broadcast tower to promote industry, jobs
item.description: The nation's top communications regulator made a stop in Baldwin County Monday morning and you might be surprised why.
item.dc:creator: Debbie Williams, KRON4

item.title: Supreme Court orders new trial for Oklahoma death row inmate
item.description: The Supreme Court on Tuesday sided with an Oklahoma death row inmate who claimed alongside the state itself that his trial was unfair, ordering a new trial and staving off his execution.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: Denny's adding egg surcharge due to nationwide shortage
item.description: Denny's will be adding a surcharge for meals that include the use of eggs at some locations due to rising costs from a nationwide egg shortage, multiple outlets are reporting.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, KTLA 5

item.title: Musk extends deadline to describe work
item.description: More than two million federal workers remain uncertain about their job security after last night's deadline to respond to an email from Elon Musk, the man President Donald Trump has tapped to make the government more efficient.
item.dc:creator: Reshad Hudson, WGN 9

item.title: Major crafts retailer closing all stores
item.description: Less than two weeks after revealing it would close more than 500 stores, craft retailer JOANN says "going-out-of-business sales" will begin at all of its locations. JOANN filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection in January, marking the second such filing by the company in less than a year.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KTLA 5

item.title: Antivirus software company ordered to pay customer $16.5M: How to file a claim
item.description: Millions of American consumers who bought antivirus software from Avast may be eligible to receive a portion of a $16.5 million settlement, the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5

item.title: Supreme Court schedules arguments in case where Trump administration is defending ACA
item.description: The Supreme Court on Monday scheduled arguments for April 21 in a case that could decide the legality of the Affordable Care Act’s (ACA) requirement that insurers cover certain preventive services.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, WGN 9

item.title: McConnell suggests Trump has ‘gross misunderstanding’ of Ukraine talks
item.description: Former Senate Republican Leader Mitch McConnell (R-Ky.) on Monday suggested that President Trump’s refusal to blame Russia as the sole aggressor who provoked the war in Ukraine “reflects a gross misunderstanding of the nature of negotiations and leverage.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KRON4

item.title: List: Bay Area JOANN stores closing part of more than 500 shutting down nationwide
item.description: NEW YORK (AP/KRON) — Fabric and crafts retailer Joann Inc., which has been a destination for generations of quilters, knitters and lovers of crafts projects for more than 80 years, is going out of business and shuttering all its stores.
item.dc:creator: ANNE D'INNOCENZIO, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Judge blocks Department of Education, OPM from disclosing troves of sensitive personal data to DOGE
item.description: A federal judge on Monday temporarily barred the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) from accessing troves of sensitive personal data from federal agencies. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: Mystics trade 2-time All-Star Ariel Atkins to the Sky for No. 3 pick in WNBA draft
item.description: The Washington Mystics accelerated their rebuild, trading two-time All-Star Ariel Atkins to the Chicago Sky for the No. 3 overall pick in this year's draft on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW SELIGMAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: The Trump administration is firing 2,000 USAID workers and putting thousands of others on leave
item.description: The Trump administration said Sunday that it was placing all but a fraction of staffers at the U.S. Agency for International Development on leave worldwide and eliminating 2,000 U.S.-based staff positions.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Nearly 200 Venezuelan migrants are flown home from Guantanamo Bay, with a layover in Honduras
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — Nearly 200 Venezuelan immigrants to the U.S. were returned to their home country after being detained at Guantanamo Bay, in a flurry of flights that forged an unprecedented pathway for U.S. deportations. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MORGAN LEE and REGINA GARCIA CANO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: How possible USPS changes could affect your mail
item.description: “We want to have a post office that works well and doesn’t lose massive amounts of money," Trump said. “We’re thinking about doing that. And it’ll be a form of a merger, but it’ll remain the Postal Service, and I think it’ll operate a lot better.
item.dc:creator: Brad Hamilton, WGN 9

item.title: New Uber feature may overcharge you. Here's how to turn it off
item.description: Unless you disable it, a new setting in the app may end up costing you the next time you hail a ride overseas.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, ABC 4

item.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy says he'd be ready to give up presidency if it brought peace, NATO membership
item.description: “If to achieve peace, you really need me to give up my post, I’m ready,” Zelenskyy said in response to a journalist's question on whether he’d trade his office for peace. “I can trade it for NATO.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Luxury portable toilets sought after ahead of NFL Draft
item.description: The luxury restroom trailers and containers feature flushing toilets, sinks, air conditioning, and even stereo systems.
item.dc:creator: Timothy Littau, Fox 5

item.title: Lynne Marie Stewart of 'Pee-wee's Playhouse,' 'It's Always Sunny' dies: reports
item.description: The actress has recently finished filming for another season of "It's Always Sunny in Philadelphia" and her forthcoming pickleball-related film, "Dink.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Mikaela Shiffrin gets historic 100th World Cup race win and ties record for most podiums
item.description: Shiffrin’s record-extending 100th career World Cup race win Sunday fulfilled a quest put on hold by a serious crash in November.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Can you spot a credit card skimmer? What to look for
item.description: Before putting your card into a reader, the FBI says there are some easy and effective ways to protect your information.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, ABC 4

item.title: Shooting after pursuit at Air Force Base in New Mexico kills an airman and wounds another
item.description: ALBUQUERQUE, N.M. (AP) — A shooting at a U.S. Air Force base in New Mexico early Saturday left one airman dead and another wounded, military officials said, adding that it was not an act of terrorism or an attack by an outsider.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Memorial grows for Pennsylvania officer killed in hospital shooting
item.description: A memorial is growing outside the police department in West York, a borough outside of York, Pennsylvania, after officer Andrew Duarte was killed in the line of duty on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: George Stockburger, Fox 5

item.title: Briscoe tries to move past devastating penalties and Blaney to start on pole in NASCAR Atlanta race
item.description: A massive fine left Chase Briscoe facing a huge points deficit heading into Sunday's NASCAR Cup Series race at Atlanta Motor Speedway.
item.dc:creator: Charles Odum, Fox 5
